item.title: UniCredit reports record quarterly profit as investment income jumps
item.description: Italian leader pressing ahead with €35bn bid for Germany’s Commerzbank

item.title: EQT makes third offer for London-listed Intertek
item.description: Previous bids from Swedish private equity firm were rejected for ‘fundamentally undervaluing’ FTSE 100 company

item.title: Vodafone to take full control of UK mobile operator in £4.3bn deal
item.description: CK Hutchison’s decision to sell 49% stake is latest in series of asset disposals

item.title: Great expectations (for earnings)
item.description: Stocks look cheaper — if everything goes perfectly

item.title: HSBC profits hit by fraud-related credit losses
item.description: First-quarter income falls $100mn to $9.4bn, missing analysts’ expectations

item.title: FirstFT: Renewed attacks threaten US-Iran truce
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: LVMH to offload brands and Deutsche Bank lawsuit

item.title: Quantum computing moves closer to drug discovery with enzyme study
item.description: Scientists have used the technology to model the behaviour of protein molecules

item.title: Golf must be careful of soaring prize money, says head of R&A
item.description: Mark Darbon says golf must heed lessons of English rugby, where clubs raised wages beyond what revenues could support

item.title: Consumers face more pain as companies threaten price rises
item.description: Executives warn that a prolonged energy shock would increase pressure to pass on costs to customers

item.title: Can Wachtell withstand Big Law’s free-agent era?
item.description: The firm’s lockstep pay structure looks quaint as fierce rainmaker poaching wars reshape the sector

item.title: Why the Iran war might not spur a faster transition to low-carbon energy
item.description: Increased priority on security of power supply might see some countries double down on fossil fuels

item.title: Welcome to the Great Hunkering Down
item.description: Why aren’t more people quitting their jobs?

item.title: Deutsche Bank denies training bankers to manipulate markets
item.description: Former commodities trader’s £12mn lawsuit alleges German lender breached a ‘duty of care’

item.title: Shell lobbying EU over rules on how green its green hydrogen must be
item.description: Debate on power standards is playing out across industries

item.title: End of the line: why the Schroder family sold up
item.description: The sale of the UK’s largest independent asset manager comes after the death of a patriarch and the rise of giant US funds

item.title: Fortress expands in US legal market with personal injury law firm deal
item.description: Investment manager uses ownership structure designed to work around profession’s ban on outside equity capital

item.title: Arnault and LVMH go from buyer to seller as luxury’s winter drags on
item.description: LVMH is exploring sales of brands including Marc Jacobs and Fenty in one of the biggest pullbacks in its history

item.title: Solar panel prices rise after China clampdown on producer competition
item.description: Rebound may spell end of the era of ever-cheaper cells after a price war that stoked huge losses among biggest producers

item.title: ‘An existential crisis’: residents pay for Nashville boom
item.description: Tennessee policy attracts corporations and high earners but locals are being priced out of Music City

item.title: Private equity firms face reckoning as £31bn broadband push turns sour
item.description: Significant losses and consumer ‘inertia’ drive expectations of consolidation among the ‘altnet’ UK providers

item.title: EU Commission spending on energy consultants jumps 400% over decade
item.description: Reliance on external advisers has drawn criticism for potential conflicts of interest

item.title: Disney’s new CEO faces first challenge
item.description: Company reports earnings on Wednesday and its spat with Donald Trump is sure to come up

item.title: Musk and SEC propose to settle Twitter lawsuit for just $1.5mn
item.description: Deal would come at a fraction of the amount regulators originally sought from the world’s richest man

item.title: Citigroup sets new rewards structure for banking and wealth referrals
item.description: Employees will be paid directly for generating business instead of sharing revenue between the group’s units

item.title: Rheinmetall’s first-quarter revenues fail to meet expectations
item.description: German company’s shares under pressure over profitability concerns and questions about the future of warfare

item.title: ‘Unprecedented’: transportation costs for US World Cup games have fans crying foul
item.description: Soaring price of heading to New Jersey and Boston stadiums adds to expense of watching this summer’s tournament

item.title: Ebay weighs GameStop’s unsolicited $56bn offer for online marketplace
item.description: Video game retailer offers $125 a share for resale web group with plans to bring in ‘entrepreneurial mindset’

item.title: Musk threatened to make OpenAI’s Altman and Brockman ‘the most hated men in America’
item.description: Text exchange revealed in court filings came after world’s richest man tried to start last-minute settlement talks

item.title: The oil price crunch is looming
item.description: The longer the Strait of Hormuz blockade continues, the more likely a crisis-like adjustment in rich economies becomes

item.title: Trump family crypto venture sues major backer for defamation
item.description: World Liberty Financial accusation against Justin Sun comes after he sues firm over alleged fraud
